content.problemStatement:
Ada has a set of NNN non-negative integers S={x1,x2,…,xN}S = \{x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_N\}S={x1​,x2​,…,xN​}. She defined the function WWW as follows:


W({})=0W(\{\}) = 0W({})=0.
If S={a}S = \{a\}S={a}, then W(S)=aW(S) = aW(S)=a.
If S={a,b}S = \{a, b\}S={a,b} (so ∣S∣=2|S| = 2∣S∣=2), then W(S)=(a⊕b)−1W(S) = (a \oplus b) - 1W(S)=(a⊕b)−1.
If ∣S∣≥3|S| \ge 3∣S∣≥3, then to calculate W(S)W(S)W(S), find any two elements of SSS that are congruent (have the same remainders) modulo the highest possible power of 222 (the highest power for which such a pair exists). Let's denote them by aaa and bbb. Then W(S)=W({a,b})⊕W(S∖{a,b})W(S) = W(\{a, b\}) \oplus W(S \setminus \{a, b\})W(S)=W({a,b})⊕W(S∖{a,b}).
It is guaranteed that W(S)W(S)W(S) is well-defined, i.e. does not depend on the exact choice of the two congruent elements.
Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ represents the binary XOR operation and A∖BA \setminus BA∖B represents set difference.

Ada needs to calculate the function WWW for each set that is created by removing exactly one element from SSS. Formally, she wants to find W(S∖{xi})W(S \setminus \{x_i\})W(S∖{xi​}) for each valid iii. Can you help her?
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The next line contains N space-separated integers x1, x2, …, xN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing N space-separated integers. For each valid i, the i-th of these integers should denote W(S∖{xi}).

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each element in the set, we calculate W(S∖{xi}) and print the results.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to understand the definition of the function W(S) and how it is calculated. We will use a recursive approach to calculate W(S) for each set S. We will also use the properties of the XOR operation to simplify the calculations.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * log(max(xi))) — explain: per query is O(N * log(max(xi))), total is O(T * N * log(max(xi)))
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — explain: we need to store the set S and the results of the recursive calls

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
# Uses input() or sys.stdin
# Handles all T test cases
import sys

def calculateW(arr, n):
    if n == 0: return 0
    if n == 1: return arr[0]
    if n == 2: return (arr[0] ^ arr[1]) - 1
    # find two elements that are congruent modulo the highest possible power of 2
    maxPower = 0
    while (1 << (maxPower + 1)) <= arr[0]: maxPower += 1
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(i + 1, n):
            if (arr[i] & ((1 << maxPower) - 1)) == (arr[j] & ((1 << maxPower) - 1)):
                a, b = arr[i], arr[j]
                new_arr = [arr[k] for k in range(n) if k not in (i, j)]
                return (a ^ b) - 1 ^ calculateW(new_arr, n - 2)
    return -1  # should not reach here

T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    for i in range(N):
        new_arr = [arr[j] for j in range(N) if j != i]
        print(calculateW(new_arr, N - 1), end=' ')
    print()
